desc:
This sturdy little bridge is cleverly built: obvious Dwarf-work. This much iron
can only have come from their mines in the Blue Mountains which loom in the
western distance, high above the flat expanse of the River Lhun. From below you
comes the murmur of moving water and the rustling of reeds in the breeze.
name: Iron Bridge

desc:
You are walking on a trail which narrows in front of a medium sized bridge. To
the west of you is a river, to the east you see a small branch of the river with
grassy green shores. You are amazed by the abundance of the wildlife here. You
feel so close to nature! There is a delicate slope all the way down to the
river.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
To the west of you a clear river is flowing peacefully, its banks lined with
bullrushes and willow trees. You are soothed by its freshness. Fish seem to
enjoy themselves, jumping above the water level as if they were playing. The
trail follows the riverbank north and south.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The worn trail continues north and south from here. To the west you can see the
river flowing gently southwards. In some places the trail appears to have been
damaged during storms long ago.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Here the worn trail turns north and west. To the east, you see some carvings in
a stone wall which might deserve closer examination.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
The worn trail bends south and east here, with the river to the north and west.
Some young birches grow by the side of the trail. There is a strong, yet fresh
smell of swiftly flowing water here, slightly spiced with the air of the
surrounding forests.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
The worn trail continues north and south here. To the east, you can see beyond
some valleys, but unfortunately the passage there is blocked off by overgrown
bushes. To the west, you can hear what sounds like a river lightly flowing.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail continues still further north and south. Only a nearby small bridge
beyond south seems interesting enough to break the trail's monotony.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Here you see a small path leading eastwards. As you look closer, you see that
the path goes in the direction of a small forest to the east. The trail
continuous north and south from here, for as far as you can see. The calm river
is west of here.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
North and south, the trail continues its journey between the road to the Grey
Havens, and the far distant peaks of the Blue Mountains. To the west lies the
coastline of the Gulf of Lhun, but from here it is not possible to reach those
sparkling waters, for the banks stand too steep to scale here.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Continuing north and south, the worn trail here offers you pleasant views of the
Gulf of Lhun. Although the water cannot be reached from this vantage point,
perhaps following the trail might take you to a place where you could step to
the water's edge.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The worn trail continues north and south here. To the west you see the river
flowing gently south, though you can not enter it from here. The trail looks
like many use it daily, and is in a good condition. There's a cold wind coming
from the north here. In the distant north you can see some forest.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The worn trail continues north and east from here. To the west is what seems to
be a gentle river, although some sounds from further north might imply that it
isn't so gentle. To the east, you see some bushes that seem to offer more than
what you see at their surface. South, you can see a natural harbour rising up.
You must be close to the Grey Havens.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
You have entered a series of bushes which seem to go on for quite a long while.
To your north, you can continue through them, or you can return to the trail
back west. To the south you can catch the sight of the mighty delta of the river
Lhun. The ground there looks swampy.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The path continues here on level ground with the shore to the west. The ground
is mostly earth with few rocks and some bushy vegetation that grows thicker to
the east. The waters appear calm and it would probably be safe to enter them
directly west. The worn path continues both to the north and south.
name: A Worn Path

desc:
The ground here slopes down gradually and appears to reach sea level further
north. The sound of the ocean can be heard in the western distance and a strong
breeze carries the scents of the water. Small stones of various sizes cover the
area and a few boulders have been moved to create a wide path leading north and
west. Spare vegetation grows along the path and sandy ground lies eastward.
name: Bend in a Path

desc:
The elevated ground upon which this path has been established ends in a nearly
vertical drop directly north of here. Waves continuously batter the western
cliffs resulting in the frequent drenching of anything existing in this area. No
vegetation has been able to maintain itself upon these smooth cliffs and the
sounds of breaking waves are deafening. Traversable ground continues eastward
and to the south.
name: Bend in the Cliff Path

desc:
Sprays of water erupt as waves crash into solid cliffs below you to the west.
The ground is covered with smooth and uneven rock plates that have become
slippery from the constant rain of sea water upon them. The path is by no means
straight as it bends around gigantic rock formations reaching up from the sea
level. To the east the solid rockface ends and gives way to boulders and some
sand. This path continues northwards and south onto a wooden structure.
name: Path above the Cliffs

desc:
The bridge has been constructed to easily support a heavy load. It is built from
logs tied tightly together and cut to provide a flat surface. Water can be heard
gurgling its way northwest into the sea. A small path leads north onto rocky
cliffs while an impressive iron gate rises up directly west. A well- travelled
road continues away from the gate and off of the bridge to the south.
name: Bridge before the Grey Havens

desc:
Bending to west and south, the tributary continues, soon to meet its
destination. The strong current could easily sweep you away. To the west you can
see a small delta with a heavy iron bridge leaping over it. On the northern and
western shores of the stream a forest of scattered pine trees rises, but the
banks are too steep for you to leave the stream.
name: A Bend in the Tributary

desc:
Bending to the north, the tributary continues, soon to meet it's destination.
The strong current could easily sweep you away, you better be careful. To the
north the stream bends westwards again.
name: A Bend in the Tributary

desc:
Your steps on the trunk are cautious, even though its firmly placed. One never
knows when its going to shake, and the waters below seem quite hostile for a
swimmer. This trunk must have come from the forest to the north. South of here,
you see a muddy path which is barely noticeable in the thick soil. The river
runs down from here, however there is no way to reach it.
name: A Trunk Bridge over the Tributary

desc:
The tributary grows wider as it continues to the west. The water is very deep
and it's impossible to penetrate. Small sticks and straws are floating on the
surface. To the north you can see a small path leading into a sparse pine
forest. You see another path to the south on the muddy shore.
name: Tributary of the River

desc:
The tributary changes its direction here and continues to the west. The current
makes the water mix with sand and small rocks. It's impossible to see the
bottom. To the north you see a sparse pine forest.
name: Tributary of the River

desc:
The tributary grows larger as it flows to the north. The current is getting
stronger and forces you downstreams. To the west you see a small, muddy path.
name: Tributary of the River

desc:
The tributary is just a small stream here, striving to the west. You can easily
see the sandy bottom beneath you. To the north you can see the border of the
tributary.
name: Tributary of the River

desc:
You trod along this hidden path that leads to different parts of the worn trail.
To the south you see a lush green valley, but unfortunately the path does not
lead there. A promontory of tall rocks makes it impossible to advance to the
west.
name: Shortcut

desc:
You are continuing on this shortcut that leads between parts of the worn trail.
You consider what could be south of this shortcut, but you also keenly reason
that you should stay on the major trail if you are travelling in haste.
name: Shortcut

desc:
You continue on this path that continues east-west. Although you know you can
return to the worn trail, you notice up ahead a passageway to the south that
might connect to the lush valleys you've been noticing.
name: Shortcut

desc:
This delightful safe passageway allows you to go between the worn trail heading
towards Grey Havens and the worn trail heading towards the Dwarven Home. You can
move south or west along this path.
name: Shortcut

desc:
The worn trail bends to the north and east here, and you notice some overgrown
farmlands to your south. To the far south, you can see what might be a safe
haven, an abandoned farmhouse.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
The wind seems to pick up here, adding to the aridness of the plains, yet you
move on. To the east you can see what was once a prosperous farmhouse, and you
can see more plains to the west.
name: Plains

desc:
The farmlands stretch along here, and you savor the light wind coming from the
valleys in the distant west. You feel calm and safe here as the farmlands
stretch further west and east, with a farmhouse to the south.
name: Overgrown Farmlands

desc:
Strangely, your exploring has taken you to a dead end. The farmlands about you
seem serene, yet there might be a small danger. After peering north, you see a
small path, and to the west, you see a large, unnaturally overgrown bush.
name: Overgrown Farmlands

desc:
You have entered what appears to be a series of colourful valleys. This lush,
green valley proves to be plentiful for all of its inhabitants. You can continue
east-west here, but you feel at such ease that you might stay a little longer.
name: Green Valley

desc:
You breathe in the clean, fresh air, as you look around and assess the
surroundings. The valley stretches beautifully before you, with small clumps of
trees, and a comfortable padding of grass underneath your feet adds to the
general welcoming feeling here. As you wonder how much longer your luck will
last here, you notice the once burned part of the valley further west.
name: Green Valley

desc:
All around you are the different shades of green: light green leaves, lush dark
grass turf and yellowish green bushes and small plants. You can continue further
west to what appears to be a burned out valley, or you can go east to the worn
trail.
name: Green Valley

desc:
You bend over to examine the remains of a huge burned tree trunk, and count the
number of circles to see the tree's age. You suddenly feel very young in
comparison to the age of the land around you, and wonder what it might have once
been.
name: Burned Valley

desc:
You find yourself at the end of a valley. Here the burned out lands to the west
meet a slightly more hospitable, but nevertheless dry and rough area of plains.
In the distance to the east, a small building can faintly be seen.
name: Plains

desc:
Dry weeds nip at your heels as you make your way through the plains. The air
seems annoyingly dry and you can hear wild animals howling in the distance. You
can continue west or east from here.
name: Plains

desc:
The solitariness of the plains seems both foreboding and inviting. All around
you are small growths of vegetation, and you wonder if you will see the plush
greenness that you remember of your hometown. You can continue east or west
here.
name: Plains

desc:
The terrain changes in sharp contrast here. The absence of trees and the sharp
wind catalyzed the drying of this once lush valley. Further south, you see the
remains of a valley once burned, and to the east you feel welcomed by the semi-
agrarian setting.
name: Dry Valley

desc:
You can't help but to wonder what might have once happened here. Blackened trees
and their decay litters the ground as you gingerly step around the young and
struggling green sprouts. You can smell hints of the disaster as small wafts of
air bring the smouldering smoke smell to your nose.
name: Burned Valley

desc:
The worn trail continues north and east from here. Following the path north will
probably take you to the Dwarven Homes, but you wonder about visiting the
twinkling land of the Elves at Grey Havens, that happens to be somewhere to the
south. You notice some bushes in that direction... perhaps it will take you on a
more preferable path.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
You are standing on a path full of mud! Every step gets some more mud on your
boots causing you to slip frequently. You hear a stream to the north and east,
where you see a river that cannot be crossed without a boat. To the west you see
the path becoming even more muddy, and to the south path becomes dry.
name: Muddy Path

desc:
To the north and south of here, the ground becomes a complete quagmire, quite
impossible to pass. However, east and west, a narrow paths plies its way through
the oozing mud. In the distance a small river can be seen, travelling slowly
westward.
name: Muddy Path

desc:
The ground here is soft and it feels as if it may give way under your feet.
Although there doesn't seem to be anything that could threaten your life here,
journeys of the past have often proven such feelings wrong. Some kind of danger
always lurks around. To the north you see a trunk spanning the river. Strong and
firm, it is the only way to cross the waters.
name: Muddy Path

desc:
This crickety barn doesn't look like it will last much longer without a
caretaker. Although most of the paint has peeled off, you see that it was once
painted green and red, and was probably quite elaborate for a common farmhouse
in its day.
name: Abandoned Farmhouse

desc:
You are standing in what apparently was once a lush farmland. Overgrown
wheatfields and cornfields dot the land before a tall yet rickety building to
your south.
name: Overgrown Farmlands

desc:
The dry, dusty, yet dependable worn trail continues here. To your south, you see
the remains of what was once a fruitful farm, and to the west the worn trail
continues further.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail bends here west and south, and you trod happily in anticipation of the
delightful cities before you. To the distant east, you see unpassable
grasslands, but you end your gaze at the colourful valleys to your west.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
The trail goes straight from the east to west. To the north and south thorny
bushes grow so thick that leaving the trail is not possible. The ground is
evenly descending westward. A hole can be spotted between the roots of one of
the northern bushes.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The worn trail continues east and west, bordered by thick thorny bushes that
prevent you from leaving it. The path forms a slight depression, hint that it
was commonly used in the past, although it looks rather abandoned now.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail travels east-west. The traffic on the path has been very light lately
and here and there some grass is pushing leaves between the stones on the
ground. In the south opens a large grassy area where the grass has grown about
waist-high. To the north some large bushes block your view.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The winding trail comes to an end. In the west the trail goes straight through
the plains. The trail is easy to travel and wide enough for a small wagon. In
the east a small hill slopes upward.
name: Worn Trail Turning from South to West

desc:
Long trails wear down traveler's foot and spirit , but a change in the the
scenery may make the feet forget their toil and the mind lighter. The hills fail
and the plains begin. In the west the rough grasslands spread to the horizon. In
the east the hills grow against the sky.
name: Worn Trail Past a Small Hill

desc:
Gently flowing hills flatten into low lands. All around the trail small bushes
and tall grasses grow in wild tangles. The trail turns north at a large
thornbush. Some large thickets of similar brush grow west and southwest. The
grasslands look rough, desolate and difficult to cross. Nothing seems to be
there save some wildlife.
name: Worn Trail through the Shrubby Plains

desc:
The trail winds its way between some trees and bushes. It makes a small turn
around a large boulder and continues north. South the trail starts turning east.
The ground is covered with small grasses. Northeast some thickets of willows
grow.
name: Winding Trail

desc:
Leaving the hills trail turns north. Earth becomes quickly quite flat and thorny
bushes and shrubs along with tall grasses cover the landscape. The trail is easy
to follow, but little off it begin deep grooves and rocks sizing from pebbles to
boulders, making journey slow and difficult. Northeast is a more wooded area.
name: Turning Trail

desc:
The tall cliff shades the trail. On top of it has been built an ancient defense
by a kingdom long gone. The wall is crumbled and some of the stones rest now at
the foot of the hill. Very few plants grow here and the rock is mainly bare. In
the north the vegetation gets thicker. In the west the trail starts to turn to
the north and in the east it steers to the south.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The merry sound of flowing water echoes here. A small spring gushes out of an
embankment off the road. A large pine tree grows, besides the water. The flow of
water has created a small stream and the roots of the tree grow across it. The
water is cool and clear. A large hollow tree trunk rests beside the small pool,
which has formed near the water's source, and it provides a perfect place to
rest. Some weeping willows sway in the breeze. A small foot path leads into the
woods in the east.
name: Worn Trail by a Small Spring

desc:
The trail turns northward and starts to descend down to a small glen. In the
south a chain of steep hills rises steeply above the valley. To the west some
wild bushes form an impenetrable thicket. In the east the trail cuts through the
hill side. The trail is good and the going is easy. A merry song or a whistled
tune would now be appropriate.
name: Worn Trail Descending Down to a Glen

desc:
The trail is dug into the sloping hill. It travels from the east to west. To the
north grows an impenetrable thorny hedge. The air is cold in the shadow of the
hill. In the hillside high above, some juniper bushes grow upon a small ledge.
The ground is covered with loose rocks that have fallen down from the hill.
name: Worn Trail Cut through a Hill Side

desc:
A small dusty crossroad lies nestled between surrounding hills. Three distinct
trails leave the clearing in different directions. The surrounding hills are
covered in huckleberry and other shrubs but free of trees. The terrain seems
more rugged towards the east, and the hills stand taller in that direction.
name: Diverging Trails

desc:
The trail levels between two high hills. The hillsides are steep and it isn't
possible to climb them. Some juniper bushes are growing on top of the hills. To
the north the trail starts turning west around the hill. In the south the trail
dives into a small vale.
name: Worn Trail between the Hills

desc:
The hill descends southeast to a long depression between the hills. Your nerves
are calmed and you begin to like this country as you gaze down on this golden
vale. The hills rise gently all around you forming pleasant shapes against the
sky. Tall bushes grow below the steep hill to the west.
name: Worn Trail through the Hills

desc:
Between the grassy hills a long vale stretches from east to west. Golden grass
grows thick all around the path. North the path climbs through a gap between the
hills.
name: Worn Trail across a Long Vale

desc:
This lovely path winds itself through the rolling hills all around. To the east
grow large flowering bushes and to the west a small hill invites you to take
look at the surrounding countryside. The path descends down to a small vale
between the hills.
name: Worn Trail through the Hills

desc:
The trail twists its way along the Tower Hills. To the east the hills become
larger and leaving the trail is not easy. Some grass grows in the fine cracks of
the rock and some small bushes grow along the side of the path. To the west you
hear water murmur; in all other directions the hills continue.
name: Trail through the Hills

desc:
The forest seems to open here as it reaches the river. The stream is small, but
the waters are deep enough so you need a boat of some kind. However a strong
trunk cut from the forest lays between the shores, making it possible to walk
through without problem. The path becomes tinier where it enters the forest to
the east. To the north the trees make a dark barrier, but you can explore in
that direction.
name: Path in the Sparse Pine Forest

desc:
The path faints as it enters the forest, devoured by the number of pines
towering randomly all around you. However, you see a trace of the path leading
both east and west. To the north the forest becomes darker, but hardly
dangerous. Some noises tell you that you are not alone.
name: Path in the Sparse Pine Forest

desc:
The forest opens itself here, allowing the sky to be seen through the thick
branches. To the south a small river flows gently, but a boat is certainly
needed to enter the waters. The path continues to the west and the north. A dim
path goes east and west.
name: Path in the Sparse Pine Forest

desc:
The forest opens itself here, causing the sky to be visible through the thick
branches. To the south a small river flows gently, but a boat is certainly
necessary for one to enter the waters. The path continues to the west.
name: Path in the Sparse Pine Forest

desc:
You are on a delta under an impressive iron bridge. This is where a stream
empties into the mighty River Lhun. Bullrushes grow all about. To the east you
can follow the creek upstream, although the current is quite strong. In the west
the Lhun flows from north to south. It is possible to climb up to the bridge
above you.
name: Delta of the Tributary

desc:
You have entered into a musty part of the farmhouse. Cobwebs lie around you and
you sneeze as you breathe in the dust. Although it seems to have been abandoned
for quite some time, you speculate that it may still be inhabited by strange
creatures.
name: Loft

desc:
You enter a vast, desolate plain. It seems endless and the dusty, dry wind
cracks your parched lips. Just looking around makes your mouth thirst, and you
clench your skin, happy to see that it is still full.
name: Plains

desc:
The plains stretch on. You wonder why you entered this continuation of boredom
and what might be your last contact with civilization. Begrudgingly, you
continue, and hope that the terrain changes soon for your imagination's sake.
name: Plains

desc:
You consider that you may have been wrong as you look around you. Barely any
vegetation or signs of life are evidenced here. You decide to continue further,
and hope for the best.
name: Plains

desc:
You find yourself in a damp cave. Studying the walls, you see a number of wide
clefts in the rock, which run from floor to ceiling, and indeed it seems that
the hole to the east, and the narrow tunnel to the north are little more than
enlargements of such crevices. The floor of the chamber is muddy, and scored
with innumerable scratches, perhaps made by the claws of some mighty beast.
name: A Damp Cave

desc:
The damp smell in this room makes your nose twitch. Little bugs scamper around
the unkept room and the floor is sticky from moist dust. You notice a chest in
the southern corner, a faded and rusty black colour.
name: Basement

desc:
You have discovered what was probably a tunnel used to hide precious objects and
notorious thieves. The passageway is dark, but you can peer south to see that
the tunnel continues.
name: Hidden Tunnel

desc:
This hidden tunnel seems rather clean, and although it is dark, you can see that
there is a glimmer of light at the end of this hall. You gingerly foot your way
through and hope for the best.
name: Hidden Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel continues directly south here, and you notice a dry wind blowing in
from the south. You wonder what might have happened here, as you notice that the
debris on the floor seems to be of dried wood and plants.
name: Hidden Tunnel

desc:
You can see that the light indeed does lead to the outside, and in your rush you
nearly neglect to examine the floor coverings more carefully. Taking a deep
breath, you see some tracks of a large animal.
name: Hidden Tunnel

desc:
The epuriation of life all around you fills your parched imagination. Ponder
over this, for these thoughts can only occur in such desolate places as these
plains.
name: Plains

desc:
Brown dust and dirt covers the cracked ground. You begin to wonder what might
have cursed this land to be so barren and lifeless. Far off in the distant west,
however, you notice a bit of green.
name: Plains

desc:
You begin to wonder if there is any obliquity to these plains. Rubbing your eyes
in vain, your lips purse into a smile as you realise that there are some greens
off in the distant west.
name: Plains

desc:
Flat plains stretch in all directions providing little change of scenery for
miles around. The grass here is short and brownish probably because of poor soil
conditions and little water. Some hardy blue flowers have managed to grow here,
but their presence does little to brighten up the bleak landscape. To the south
the plains seem a shade greener than here.
name: Plains

desc:
Some large boulders have rolled off the mound. Between them some yellowish grass
grows, but no shrubs or flowers. The wind whistles and sighs between the rocks
like a whisper from elder days. It is not clear whether the message is good or
evil. Maybe the ancient spell that still lingers here has been diluted by time
to just that, a mere whisper of the wind.
name: Between the Boulders

desc:
A large mound is piled high from rough-hewn boulders to the east. The rough
lands lie open in all other directions. Some nearly lifeless thorny bushes are
scattered across the bleak landscape. Birds are quiet and there seems to be no
animals wandering near this place.
name: A Bleak Plain

desc:
Vegetation is sparse on this hard and uneven ground made of sharp-cornered
stones and earth, only some dark moss. A pretty large mound of rocks and
boulders is piled to the southeast of here. It's possible to walk along the
mound to the south and east.
name: A Rough Plain

desc:
Small rock shards poke here and there among the sparse and sometimes mottled
yellow grass, which is the only vegetation here. Dense sharp bushes prevent you
from going east, and this natural wall continues further north.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
Under the somewhat underfed wild grass, the soil is mixed with some pebbles. A
few weeds bring somber patches of colour in the landscape. The dense thicket
that continues southwards blocks your way east. Similar obstacles can be seen in
the distance, to the north and the south-west.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
The ground is covered by sharp weeds and strewn with small shrubs, making travel
painful for anyone lacking boots or thick skin. To the north a great thicket of
thorny bushes grows. Some kind of building lies west of this vegetal barrier.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
Grass and shrubs grow wild all around. The ground is broken making traveling
difficult. Here and there among the grass large stones stick out like giant
heads. To the south and northwest a large thicket of thorny bushes grow. An
especially large rock stands here, perched on top of it, a vacant hawk's nest.
East the grasslands opens south and north. Far behind them the hills grow tall.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
This dense thicket is surrounded by man-high thorny bushes. The air is stale, as
wind can not penetrate this far into the bushes. The branches that are reaching
towards the sky forms a canopy, making this a natural shelter. A rough path
coming from the east allows access to a slight depression of bare earth.
name: Inside a Dense Thicket

desc:
Grass and shrubs grow wild all around. The ground is broken making traveling
difficult. Here and there among the grass a large stone sticks out like a giant
head. Some thorny bushes grow northwest. A small bird maybe a finch sings
merrily somewhere among the shrubs.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
Grass and shrubs grow wild all around. The ground is broken making traveling
difficult. Thick thorny bushes grow middle of some rocks and shrubs. In the
south they become so thick that entering them is impossible. In the east rough
grasses and shrubs continue. Little ways off in the northwest is another
thicket.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
Many holes in the ground make footing treacherous. Many rocks and dense shrubs
slow down progress through these desolate plains. Looking closer however the
grasses and shrubs are teaming with insect life and many birds fly around
catching them. Some bushes grow here and there, and behind them plains seem to
continue every direction.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
Many holes in the ground make footing treacherous. Many rocks and dense shrubs
slow down progress through these desolate plains. Looking closer however the
grasses and shrubs are teaming with insect life and many birds fly around
catching them. Some bushes grow here and there, and behind them plains seem to
continue every direction.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
Many holes in the ground make footing treacherous. Many rocks and dense shrubs
slow down progress through these desolate plains. Looking closer however the
grasses and shrubs are teaming with insect life and many birds fly around
catching them. Some bushes grow here and there, and behind them plains seem to
continue every direction.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
While the outer vegetation of this thicket is rather low and dense, the shrubs
here in its centre grow much higher, reaching for the dim light above. The bare
stems near the ground level allow an easy passage for any wildlife around here.
Large patches of earth have been overturned, uprooting the vegetation that grew
there and creating a clearing in the thicket.
name: Clearing inside a Dense Thicket

desc:
Some bushes to dodge and lots of rocks and holes to hop over. Those elements
dominate these rough plains. In the northwest the bushes become impenetrable and
to the southwest is a sinister-looking pile of boulders. However there is some
open ground between the boulders and bush and with some determination one might
be able to pass through. To the east the land is open until the hills start to
rise taller and taller.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
Many holes in the ground make footing treacherous. Many rocks and dense shrubs
slow down progress through these desolate plains. Looking closer however the
grasses and shrubs are teaming with insect life and many birds fly around
catching them. Some bushes grow here and there, and behind them plains seem to
continue every direction.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
Some bushes grow here providing hiding places for small creatures that hide
before you can see them. The hill side east of here is almost vertical. South,
west and north the flat ground continues.
name: Bushes below the Cliff

desc:
Some bushes grow here. Square pieces of rock lie on the ground. The hill side
east of here is almost vertical. South, west and north the flat ground
continues.
name: Bushes below the Cliff

desc:
Some bushes grow here. Square pieces of rock lie on the ground. The hill side
east of here is almost vertical. South and west the flat ground continues. To
the north a path winds its way through the rough plains.
name: Bushes below the Cliff

desc:
The ground is very broken here. Stones and grasses make traveling tiring and
unpleasant. Some large boulders have rolled down the hill northeast long time
ago.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
Many holes in the ground make footing treacherous. Many rocks and dense shrubs
slow down progress through these desolate plains. Looking closer however the
grasses and shrubs are teaming with insect life and many birds fly around
catching them. Some bushes grow here and there, and behind them plains open to
the north, east and west. South and southwest a small hill rises above the
plains.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
Grass and shrubs grow wild all around. The ground is broken making traveling
difficult. Here and there among the grass large stones stick out like giant
heads. In the west an enormous pile of rock rises from the plains. It is almost
size of a small hill. In the south is a small hill. Grasslands continue in other
directions.
name: Rough Grasslands

desc:
The hill drops down steeply to the east and north. Small flowers dot the ground
around an old twisted tree. The land to the north descend in gentle waves
westward. Large thickets of some dark bushes stand out like islands middle of
the golden sea. Many birds fly in flocks above the plains, and high above them
almost out sight a lone hawk circles looking for his prey. In the east is
another hill top. Down below the drop little ways to the north a large mound of
some kind is built.
name: Last Hill

desc:
This appears to be the ruin of an old outpost. After examining the place you
discover that this was a dwarven outpost, but nothing is left except from a few
broken stone tables. The floor here is filled with rubble from the roof and the
walls.
name: Old Ruins

desc:
The plains in this area seem to change into a more desolate area as you look to
the east and northeast. The plains continues south as far as your eyes can see.
West of here a very tall and thick brush can be seen. While looking north you
suddenly notice what appears to be some very old ruins.
name: Plains

desc:
You bend down to peer at a small thorny bush. Its endurance in harsh conditions
inspires you to hope that the harsh plains will offer you something to gain. The
greens to the west seem to be the growths on the outside of a rock.
name: Plains

desc:
These plains seem to stretch on endlessly. The ground is hard, composed mainly
of sand and rock, but this does not seem to deter the abundant grasses and weeds
that grow everywhere. A few spots of colour are visible where wildflowers manage
to survive.
name: Plains

desc:
Flat, treeless land continues in all directions with the exception of an
extremely thorny section of bush to the west. Travel in that direction would
result in the skin of the creature attempting the passage being ripped to shreds
by the strong, sharp thorns sticking from the branches on the bushes.
name: Plains

desc:
Certainly this was built for men of ages past. There is no entry to this
structure. Tedious must have been the labor to build such housings. No doorway
is visible anywhere. Some boulders larger than others stick out of the pile like
spikes from a morningstar. There is an ominous feeling in the air, like a sudden
thunderstorm that has been brewing from the beginning of time is about to
strike. Stone black as night stands on the side of the mound. Strange whispers
echo in the air. Tales of fear and terror beyond words come to mind to those who
listen.
name: Rocky Mound

desc:
Large berry bushes grow wild here. To the north they become increasingly
thicker, until a point where they are far to dense to allow one to see what lies
behind. To the south the bushes have failed to grow as thickly. Going that way
might make for easier travelling.
name: Brushwood

desc:
The hill slopes down eastward. Grass grows tall upon its shoulders, and here and
there small white and yellow flowers sway in the breeze. The lands to the north
descend in gentle waves westward. Large thickets of some dark bushes stand out
like islands in the middle of the golden sea. Many birds fly in flocks above the
plains, and high above them almost out sight a lone hawk circles looking for his
prey. West is another hill top.
name: Last Hill

desc:
The ground slopes a bit to the west and south. Grasses grow shorter here than to
the north where the land is much rougher. A tall row of evergreen bushes grows
to the south. West is a long low hill and east seems to be a small field of some
kind.
name: Tall Hedge

desc:
The grass here is thick and healthy, but does not grow quite as high as in the
surrounding areas. Finding oneself walking amongst thousands of small white
flowers, which merrily bow in the breeze, ones heart becomes content and dark
thoughts once so close vanish into thin air. Don't you wish, that this was the
strawberry season, because that is what these little plants are. Ground slopes
down from west to east. Northward the various bushes and shrubs make travel more
difficult.
name: Wild Strawberry Field

desc:
A small tree grows here next to a strange shaped stone. To the south the Tower
hills continue. To the west rough plains extend far and wide. And beyond,
somewhere far in the distance, enveloped in the mists is the Blue Mountain range
where rumor tells dwarves still dwell. To the north and east are many hills,
which are covered by shrubs and stones. Down below to the north is a long narrow
vale, which pokes like a finger from the open lands in the west into the hills
in the east. The landscape has a rough and wild look.
name: On the Hill

desc:
The hills start growing in the east. To the north and west the valley continues.
In the south a steep hillside stops you from taking that course. West is also a
path through the hills.
name: Eastern Edge of the Vale

desc:
The flowering shrubs sway gently in the wind. The hills begin to grow taller as
you move to the north or west.
name: Narrow Vale

desc:
Rocks sizing from pebbles to large rocks make the slopes more difficult to
descend or ascend. The brushwood spreads its fragrant smell all around you. To
the north is a bit taller hill and to the west is a path, but the slope is too
steep for a safe climb. In the south it is possible to descend into a narrow
vale. In the east the hills continue far into the horizon.
name: Hill Covered with Shrubs and Stones

desc:
The hill slopes steadily down into a small glade, in the west, between the
hills. The hills are coming to an end. A small mound is still to the west but
main part of the hills are to the south and east. The ground is covered by many
interesting rocks and shrubs.
name: End of a Hill

desc:
Large rocks lie on the ground. Wild bushes grow all around. The land is flat
except to the north where a small arm of the hill extends towards the west. The
birds and insects sing and buzz in the air, busily going about their business.
name: Grassy Patch between the Hills

desc:
Numerous large rocks and rock piles in these grasslands provide shelter and
hiding places for many small animals. Nearby is a very large one, thrice the
height of a man. It has a pair of swallows' nests, built by swallows of course,
under a small ledge.
name: Large Rocks

desc:
The tall trees grow close together everywhere, and their branches high above
cast dark shadows on the ground. Birds fly from tree to tree hunting for insects
that are also here in swarms. The sound of water comes from the west. Some tree
stumps among the thick layer of dry leaves indicate that men with axes visited
these woods a long time ago. The underbrush is mainly of ferns, which grow here
and there in the boggy depressions in the ground.
name: Small Wood

desc:
The large trees block out the heat of the sun and provide shelter from the wind
and rain. A small footpath runs from the east to west among the trees. Some
birds fly high above within the dark green foliage. The ground is covered by
some thick and wide leafed grasses, which grow in small patches from the rich
muddy soil.
name: Small Wood

desc:
A pleasant glen is formed between the embracing arms of the hills. Small berry
bushes grow here and there. South, below a tall and steep hillside, a small hut
is built. A path leads to it from the west, and other paths lead to small bushes
to the east and to a fenced area apparently set up to keep sheep or goats.
name: At a Small Hut

desc:
High above in the sky the branches of a huge oak tree swing back and forth in
the wind. It stands alone by the thickets that grow over the stream to the
south. The trunk is too smooth and slippery and the branches way too high for
any save the squirrels to climb this tree. The ground gets muddier to the south.
From the west comes the sound of water.
name: Large Oak

desc:
The hill slopes eastward down into a small flat area between the hills. Soothing
sounds of the wind moving in the grass surround you. A small berry bush grows
here among the grass. The ground opens wider to the south.
name: Small Hill

desc:
Too shallow to swim in and too narrow for boating, this small stream makes its
way from the thickets south and southeast to the more open water northwest. Some
bushes grow on the banks. West bank is drier than that in the east. Weeping
willow waves slowly in the wind on the east shore.
name: Small Stream

desc:
Here the stream sweeps gracefully past the base of a small hill, which seems to
mark the end of the Tower Hills, which give way to the plains to the west. A
narrow trail winds lazily along the banks of the stream, and small yellow
flowers grow among the lush grass.
name: Winding Waterway by a Small Hill

desc:
Tall poplars grow here like green pillars supporting the sky. Bushy willows with
silvery leaves sway in the wind. The stream flows in the east, but ground there
is soft and willows form an impenetrable thicket. Going there is senseless.
North a small hillock stands overlooking a stream.
name: Tall Trees beside the Path

desc:
The stream winds gently through the sprawling hills. Here it is very shallow,
with a bed comprised of small stones, which create a multicoloured pathway,
stable and secure for wading. To the north, the stream narrows somewhat, and
becomes a little deeper and more turbulent, but upstream, to the southeast it
appears to be smaller, suggesting that this area is located not far from the
source of the waterway. The waterway east is blocked by a thicket of willows.
name: Narrow Stream in the Hills

desc:
The stream flows quietly east of here through thickets of willows and water
plants. The ground here is very soft and your feet often sink deep into the mud.
Southward the ground gets firmer and a row of tall poplars grow, from east to
west. Behind them is a small trail among the hills. Insects swarm thick in the
air and the area provides them with plenty of places to breed in.
name: Stream Bank

desc:
Numerous willow bushes grow in the stream which is quite narrow and shallow. To
the north they form an unpassable thicket. The water is only knee high. Many
aquatic weeds and grasses poke their leaves above the surface. The bottom of the
stream is soft and muddy. The ground gets firmer eastward where a tall forest
grows. In the south the water continues upstream towards its source. In the west
willows grow on the streambank.
name: Small Stream

desc:
With the strength of ten men the bear has dug itself a small nest into the hills
side. The ground is covered by a thick layer of dry leaves. The walls are round.
The floor is flat and dry. This would be a pleasant little hole if the smell of
the bear wasn't so thick in the air. Maybe it's better to leave quickly.
name: Bear Den

desc:
The soft sandy bottom invites a weary adventurer to remove the sweaty boots and
wade in the cool waters. A tall beech grows on the west shore. The river flows
slowly from the southwest to the northeast. The river is too deep to cross
without a boat or by swimming.
name: Wading in the River

desc:
Tall grass stands on this small hill. The sun has dried the vegetation and it
now blows in the wind making soothing sounds. The battle of good and evil seems
far away. On the gentle slope a large pine tree grows alone surrounded by
grasses and shrubs.
name: Small Hill

desc:
The river flows gently through the rough wilderness. Small shrubs and trees grow
on the banks. It is time to desert your boat if you want to continue upstream.
The river shrinks to a small stream southeast, too overgrown for a boat.
name: Small River

desc:
Tall willows grow wild here, drinking the waters of the river. In the west they
became impenetrable. To the north the river continues downstream. To the south
is a small trail making its way through the wilderness.
name: Bushy River Bank

desc:
You are standing right in front of the huge east gate of the legendary city of
Grey Havens. High and marvellous, white marble-walls tower up above you. The
gate in front of you is made from the finest wood and sheathed with silver and
mithril. You are overwhelmed by the breathtaking eminence of this large Elven
seaport. Beside the road there is a sign mounted on a post.
name: Harlond East Gate

desc:
The bushes here seem to irritate your lower legs but you continue further. You
can continue north into the seemingly thickening bushes, or go back south where
the bushes seem more open.
name: Short Bushes

desc:
Here you can see the bushes thicken into a thorny thicket. You wonder what life
could have once lived here in such a confining area, but you see that you can
continue further, going east, or you can return to the trail south.
name: Short Bushes

desc:
You can sense that the bushes get thicker here as you trudge on. That sense of
adventure in you instinctively tells you that everything here is safe and
friendly. The passage through these bushes runs north-south.
name: Short Bushes

desc:
It's nothing to be worried about, you explain to yourself, as you see that the
thicket is really quite normal and harmless. You tediously make your way through
the restrictive branches as you notice that you can go south and west.
name: Thicket

desc:
You resist scratching a cut from the thorny thicket as you trudge further
through. Something, you think, must be out there. But you can only accept your
fate and continue on your way.
name: Thicket

desc:
Indeed, your presaging thoughts prove to be trustworthy. The thicket seems to
come alive at you, both irritating your skin and stinging your eyes. You worry
that if you go further, you might not be able to return. Yet, you trust your
instincts, and continue north.
name: Dense Thicket

desc:
You shiver as you hear strange animal noises about you, and you look quickly
back only to be relieved that you can still retreat south. To the north you see
that you could squeeze through the passage in the thicket, yet you are unsure
that you really should.
name: Dense Thicket

desc:
After making your way through that tiny passageway, you wonder how it was
possible for you to enter it. You hear the sound of water nearby. Maybe it's the
great river of Lhun flowing thru here? To the east you can spot a exit further
into the small bushes, while you can see a bit more dense bushes to the south.
name: Dense Thicket

desc:
Some light shines in through the cracks above you, but that doesn't make it
easier to perceive the surroundings since the air is full of a greyish mist. To
the north a black cave opens up. There is a smell of wild animals here, and not
unlikely it emanates from some big predator.
name: Misty Cave

desc:
This cave would otherwise be completely dark if it were not for your light
source. Travellers who successfully made their way past here have left their
marks on the cave wall. You carefully step around the huge boulders in the cave,
noticing a musty smell coming from the north, and what seems to be a light mist
to the south.
name: Black Cave

desc:
You enter a cave covered in moss and light fungus growing on the sides. The
musty smell is hardly bearable, and your boots squish on the slime underneath.
To the south you see an impending continuation of the cave.
name: Moist Cave

desc:
It's very hard to find your way through this thick shrubbery. The bushes seem to
grow thinner to the north, while to the west they thicken drastically. The way
east is effectively blocked by a sheer rock, covered by thorny bushes. To the
south the thicket seems somewhat yielding, almost elastic.
name: Bushes

desc:
West and East of here the hazels grow high, making it impossible to journey in
those directions. To the north one can barely hear the trickle of a small forest
stream. Further south, the dark yet yielding bushes extend further. The ground
is covered with brown vegetation. The only visible parts of the soil are the
black rocks which stick up above the bushes.
name: Small Bushes

desc:
The trees get denser here, and the stream has become smaller and smaller. There
is no undergrowth here, just the trees above you and some rotting materials on
the ground. To the west the stream flows silently towards the Lhun. Travel north
and east is blocked by some thick shrubbery, while the thicket looks less dense
to the south.
name: Small Stream

desc:
The trees around you loom overhead as the sky above suddenly disappears. The
water here is shallow enough to easily wade through. There is almost no
undergrowth around the stream, since the towering trees allow no sunlight to
reach the ground. One can feel a smell of decay to the east, while to the west
the air smells fresher.
name: Small Stream

desc:
A shallow stream flows lazily through the forest. Around it, the trees grow high
and thick, casting a thick shade on the ground. The water itself flows from east
to west in a steady pace. Eastward the trees grow thicker together, while to the
west the path seems lighter.
name: Forest Stream

desc:
As you walk along the small path you cant help to notice a small and peaceful
forest a little east of you. Maybe it would be a good place to enter while
taking a rest. To the west you see the worn trail and beyond the trail is the
river.
name: Small Path

desc:
You have entered, what appears to be, a frequently used resting place. Quite a
few trees surround this campsite giving you shelter from the wind. It would
appear that quite a few adventures have found this the perfect place to make
camp for the night. Between a few rocks you see a small spring flowing north.
Several branches have been burned here. The plains continues in all directions
from here. You can use this place to sleep and to refresh yourself at the
spring.
name: Campsite

desc:
You delightfully look upon this small red wooden home, which might have once
housed a hunter or another protector of the land. A makeshift table and bed once
provided lodging, and can do so once again for your own choosing.
name: Shelter

desc:
A number of bushes have been able to find a hold within the sand that covers the
ground here. Though the area is mostly sand, a number of small boulders have
lodged themselves deep into the earth and would likely be difficult to move.
Instead of the sand having been blown flat from the sea breeze reaching it from
the west, it has landed in piles making this ground uneven. A predominately
sandy region continues to the south while the bushes increase northwards.
name: Uneven Ground

desc:
Luscious grass grows amongst small thorny bushes on the slopes that rise
gradually to the north and south. A path of pressed down vegetation continues
further east along the floor of the valley. Though you could scramble up the
valley's edge to the south, northwards a high rock wall makes passage
impossible.
name: A Small Valley

desc:
A variety of grasses grow here abundantly though some of them have been
flattened by a heavy creature walking through here frequently. The valley ends
by a cluster of thornbushes in the east. Southwards the valley walls are quite
steep. A rock wall at the northern edge prevents you from travelling in that
direction.
name: A Small Valley

desc:
Thorny bushes completely surround you on all sides except for a small path
leading through them to the west. Pieces of brown fur have been caught on some
of the thorns and sway gently back and forth in a light breeze. A small
depression dug into the ground is covered with broken and gnawed bones.
name: A Dark Den

desc:
Gently sloping ground northwards leads down into a small valley. Here the earth
is covered with a thick blanket of dark green grass. A piece of driftwood has
somehow ended up on this field. The grass becomes much thinner as it encounters
less fertile ground westwards. Some fresh dug up earth lies on the ground near a
small hill to the east.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
In sharp contrast to the rocky regions west of here, the ground is mostly
covered in sand leaving it soft and hard to walk on. Interspersed among the sand
are a few rockslabs that must stem from the cliffs to the west. The sand
continues to the north and south and some of it has travelled east on the brisk
wind. Sounds of the sea reach you from the distant west.
name: Sandy Ground

desc:
The ground here is covered mostly with rock plates and a number of giant
boulders. Sand has found its way between every crack and crevice among the
rocks. A strong breeze blows here from the west and carries with it the salty
scent of a large body of water. Sounds of waves hitting shore and the cries of
gulls fill the air.
name: Rocky Ground

desc:
This region is neither all sand nor all rocks. A fast flowing stream cuts off
the area to the south, its steep and muddy shores preventing descending into the
water. Little vegetation grows here with the exception of a few hardy grasses.
Northwards the sand increases while the soil becomes softer to the east. From
the distant west you can make out the sounds of a sea.
name: Mixed Ground

desc:
Thick growth of bulrushes cover this area completely hiding the moist ground
from view. Sedges and cattails have somehow managed to find spots to grow
alongside the bulrushes. A fast flowing river runs by to the south supplying
this area with large amounts of water. A sandy region lies westwards and to the
north bushier vegetation grows.
name: On Soft Footing

desc:
A massive fire must have raged through here some time ago. Most of the original
vegetation in this area has died and what little remained of it is black and
dying. Some tiny light green grasses have begun pushing their way through the
ash surface. The remains of a tall tree lie among the ashes. There is no sign as
to how and why the fire started here.
name: Burnt Grass

desc:
The wind must have carried some burning matter onto this field from elsewhere,
as parts of this grass field have been scorched. There are only a few blackened
spots, mostly ones surrounding burnt branches. The rest of the field is covered
in a mixture of long green grass interspersed with a few golden grains.
name: Lightly Burnt Grass

desc:
Long grass can be seen all around, gently swaying in a light breeze. Parts of
the grass has been trampled down, creating small paths that branch off and take
a number of headings. The height of the grass here could easily hide numerous
creatures without them being detected. In the south the plains give way to a
swampy region of land.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The grass is fairly short here but quite thick. A thorny section of brush cuts
off the landscape to the north. A large number of dandelions have taken over
parts of the field, creating a sharp contrast between their brilliant yellow
colours and various shades of green. The fields of grass continue towards the
east and south.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
Thick grass grows in this area, mostly due to a watery region to the south that
nourishes these plains. Further north where water is less plentiful vegetation
becomes drier and less diverse. Small paths are visible leading through the
grass in various directions, their origins unknown. The uncut plains continue
both to the east and west.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
A fast flowing river cuts through the landscape here as it spews to the surface
from underground somewhere in the east and again dives down into a narrow
underground tunnel in the west. The water here is remarkably clear, revealing a
gravely bottom. Bullrushes cover a marshy ground above the tunnel westwards,
making any travel above ground there impossible. Tall trees rise empore to the
south indicating a foresty terrain. Towards the north a grassy field sways in a
light breeze.
name: On a Rushing River

desc:
The clear water flows at a fast pace to the west here. Along with small white
stones, small tussocks of lush, green grass cover the riverbank to the south. In
the north-east the highest peaks of the Tower Hills can be spotted.
name: Wild River

desc:
The cold waters of this wild river flow steadily on, indifferent to any change
of scenery on the banks. North of here one can venture into the rubble of the
beginnings of the Tower Hills. An old and proud pine forest grows on the river
bank to the south. South-east you can catch a glimpse of a high tower.
name: Wild River

desc:
Large, impressive and infertile are the hills that fill the landscape. The only
things that grow here are some small windswept bushes that somehow have managed
to get a foothold in the rocky hills. To the north and west the land changes
drastically, levelling out into vast grassy fields. A river can be seen to the
south.
name: Tower Hills

desc:
These fields are covered with short juicy grass that appears perfect for cattle
to graze in. A few bushes are scattered across the fields and an occasional tree
can be spotted here and there. To the north and west the fields spread out and
to the south a river runs wildly. Looking east, large hills fill your vision.
name: Fields

desc:
Short juicy grass covers the ground, making travel easy and comfortable. Small
animals play in the grass making all kinds of strange noises. To the south and
east large hills are rising towards the sky and to the west the grassy fields
spread out.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
Lush green grass covers the ground here and among it a few delicate flowers
grow. Though the area continues to be mostly grass to the east and south, west
of here the ground becomes less fertile. In the far north-east a forest can be
distinguished.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The earth here has completely vanished and given way to a sticky, watery mud
like substance. It is impossible to avoid sinking in here since the mud offers
little resistance. The only dry land accessible is westwards or north. Though a
bridge is present to the south any movements here only cause you to sink down
further and the distant bridge would be too high to reach. Flowing water to the
east contributes to the wetness here.
name: In a Swamp

desc:
From east to west, this wild and desolate river continues its journey. All
around, peaks of cliffs and rocks block out any possible glimpse of an unbroken
horizon - this is not the place for spectacular views! South of here there seems
to be some sort of landing.
name: Wild River

desc:
The river meanders through the hills at a steady pace, slowly finding a route
towards its ultimate goal. Between the hills to the south one can glimpse a
large tower that seems to be the only sign of habitation within this area. The
river flows on to the west, while the rocky hills that shade the water continue
to the east.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
Here among the ancient Tower Hills the wild river flows steadily on. There seems
to be little wildlife in this barren and rocky part of Arda. The river continues
its journey south. Travelling upstream to your east could be a less tiring
alternative compared to a trip into the hills themselves.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
The wild river flows steadily on, following the ancient riverbed carved deep
into the softer soil between the unyielding cliffs. The water is clear and
chilly. Travelling upstream to your east might be a less tiring alternative to a
trip into the hills themselves.
name: Wild River

desc:
The cold, clear water flows in a westerly direction. The river is quite deep,
but you can distinguish that the bottom consists of sharp rocks. To the east
high cliffs lean to the river. On the south bank, a small mountain path twists
its way among the rocky hills.
name: Bend in a Wild River

desc:
The river takes a turn here, bending from east to south. Where the river turns,
erosion has dug out large potholes in the rock. A few small bushes grow on the
riverbed; tiny fishes can be seen floating though the greenery. High sharp
cliffs tower to the west, making travelling in that direction quite hard even
for a skilled climber.
name: Bend in a Wild River

desc:
This section of the wild river seems especially barren and desolate. It would
take a skilled climber to venture south through the sharp cliffs that tower on
the riverbank. An easier option would be to follow the river downstream, which
is due west in this case. In the meantime, one could always look at the very
impressive rock formations on the bottom of the river.
name: Wild River

desc:
This dark river meanders slowly through the hills, cutting a path through the
surrounding dark cliffs and rocks. The water here is very clear and fresh, yet
it has a somewhat metallic taste. There are some signs of vegetation here: thick
weeds grow on the dramatically sloping banks.
name: Wild River

desc:
A few trees break the monotony of these hills. The trees are low and bent from
the hard wind that lashes against them. To the east a trail can be seen and to
the north a river cuts through the cliffs.
name: At Some Small Trees

desc:
Large thorny bushes are scattered among the high pointed cliffs. A strong wind
blows here, making it hard to walk upright. To the north the cliffs come to an
abrupt end as they fall steeply into a dark, wild river, while to the east the
slope isn't that steep and you would easily be able to walk down to the water.
name: Cliffs

desc:
This precipitous hillside is filled with sharp rocks and large boulders. Among
the rocks grow small tussocks of lush, green grass and small thorny bushes. To
the north the terrain becomes even more rocky and to the west the hills become
completely impassable. However, to the east, you can spot a small trail twisting
and turning through the hills.
name: Steep Hillside

desc:
A small path twists its way among the the rocky hills, speeding up travel. Large
boulders are scattered across the landscape almost as if a giant had tossed them
around. To the north you hear the roar of a wild river and to the east the hills
are so steep that travel in that direction is completely impossible.
name: A Small Rocky Path

desc:
On the top of the steep hill you can see the ruins of an old barn. It seems that
it has been left to fall apart a very long time ago. Nothing remains of the
walls except small stumps of wood pointing towards the sky, and the remains of
the roof are scattered all over the place. Grass covers most of the planks, but
some of the planks are remarkably fresh and seem to be newer than the rest of
the ruin.
name: At the Ruins of an Old Barn

desc:
Large, sharp rocks are scattered around the landscape. Between the boulders some
large thorny bushes grow so close together that they completely block the
sunlight from reaching the ground. Atop a high hilltop far to the west a tall
tower can be seen, while huge rocks completely block your way to the south.
name: Strange Shady Place

desc:
The magnificent Tower Hills stretch as far as the eye can see. There is little
vegetation here, except a few thorny bushes and some dry grass. To the west a
high, white tower reaches up to the sky.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
Long dry grass and small thorny bushes cover the hilly terrain. To the west and
north the hills fall down towards a river. The slopes are steep and rocky and
going that way will take some effort. To the east the hills open up into a rocky
wasteland. Far to the south-west you can see the contours of a high tower.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
Of all the rocky hills that surround you, this appears to be the highest. On the
top of this hill stands a high, white tower completely made of stone. It rises
several hundred feet toward the sky and the view from the top must truly be
magnificent. A zig-zagging trail, paved with small white stones, leads from the
tower's entrance to the west. Dense, thorny bushes surround the base of the
tower, making it hard to approach from anywhere but on the path.
name: At the Base of a White Tower

desc:
The dense forest completely surrounds you, hiding you from view on all sides.
The trees stand close together, making any travel here slow and dull. Forming a
thin but compact canopy above the branches block most light from reaching the
ground, giving a shadowed and grey appearance to the area. Due to the lack of
life-giving light the undergrowth here is sparse with the ground mostly covered
by fallen pine needles.
name: A Pine Forest

desc:
Here the forest opens up into a small clearing. The ground is covered with soft,
green grass which makes this a perfect place to sit down and rest. To the north
some water can be seen through the trees. To the south and east the forest
continues.
name: A Small Clearing

desc:
This probably does not even qualify to be named a bridge. Two medium sized logs
have been pulled across the river below you and tied together. The logs are
quite slippery from the water splashing up. Since the tree trunks are still
rounded, one needs to have good balance to cross the river here. The water flows
by southwards, but its origin is unknown. To the north, where there should be a
river, a swampy area looks uninviting.
name: On a Small Bridge

desc:
Instead of the familiar green colours that are usually found in this region,
most of this area is smothered by a blanket of colourful blossoms. The field is
bordered by a fast flowing stream passing by in the south. Some logs allow
crossing of part of the river to the east. Vegetation grows more coarsely north
of here and the flowers give way a field of grass westwards.
name: A Flowery Field

desc:
The riverbed widens here somewhat as water reaches it from both the south and
the north. A slope climbs upwards in the east towards a wooden region. Murky
water, likely due to the stir up created by the increased waterflow, prevents
judging the depth of the water here. The river continues at a quick pace
westwards.
name: Joining of Two Streams

desc:
The river flows quickly westwards in a nearly straight fashion. Steep slopes
rise upwards both to the north and south. It would be difficult to get a grip
anywhere on the riverbank. The water is far from clear here, it seems to be
carrying a lot of mud and sand with it from regions to the east.
name: A Fast Flowing River

desc:
Water moves here at a leisurely pace northwards where sounds suggest an increase
in the flow of the river. The stream arrives from the east and is redirected by
a riverbank to the west. The riverbank is quite muddy and any attempts to climb
it would be unsuccessful since there are no easy grips available. South a gaping
hole might indicate the presence of a passage.
name: A Slow Moving River

desc:
There is hardly a current on this river as it slowly makes its way westwards.
The river is quite clear here and a rock filled bottom is visible. To the north
stands a small forest which is easily accessible from here. The southern slope
however is muddy and would be impossible to climb.
name: Upon a Slow Moving River

desc:
Clear and tranquil, this pond has a soothing impact on the environment. The only
sound that can be heard is the chirping of small birds looking for food on the
edge of the pond. Large bushes grow around the pond making it a quiet, isolated
haven. Through the bushes to the south you can spot a large road passing by.
name: A Small Pond

desc:
Large pines grow here overshadowing your path. There seems to be almost no
undergrowth as the sunlight never reaches the ground. The dense and dark forest
continues to the east but to the north a small clearing can be seen. To the
south a pond can be spotted through the trees.
name: A Pine Forest

desc:
Light mist climbs up the western and southern riverbanks and gently blankets the
ground here. Some colourful dried leaves might have fallen from the trees a
while ago for they now cover the ground. The forest continues north and east.
Near the southern riverbank a thick patch of mushrooms has found a suitable spot
to grow.
name: A Light Forest

desc:
A small animal path is winding its way through this forest of tall pines. Some
small bushes grow beside the path and the sounds of small animals can be heard
from within them. The forest continues to the north and west but to the east the
large Tower Hills can be seen rising towards the sky.
name: A Pine Forest

desc:
The brook is quite wide here and its pace is therefore much slower than to the
south where the brook narrows. The riverbanks consist of dark, moist soil
covered with lush, green grass and small white flowers. To the north a dark pine
forest can be seen. The brook flows west at a moderate pace.
name: A Large Brook

desc:
The brook forms a quite fast-paced stream here as it becomes even more narrow
than before. High cliffs prevent you from going west or south, as the rock is so
smooth that it is impossible to get a proper grip on them. The brook flows
northward at a very high pace.
name: A Large Brook

desc:
Coming from the north and smoothly turning west, the brook widens somewhat here
as it is nourished by the waters of another stream that flows in from the south.
It is hard to judge the depth of the brook as the water is murky due to the
stir-up created by the increased water-flow. A high cliff rises towards the sky
in the east.
name: A Large Brook

desc:
Large hills surround you on all sides. A small path winds its way in a northerly
direction. Some small thorny bushes are scattered around the path, making it
hard to walk beside it. To the west the hills are so steep that even a skilled
climber has no chance of going in that direction. In the far north a high, white
tower can be distinguished.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
A small brook flows in from the east and fills a hollow in the ground here,
forming a pond. As the brook never ceases its flow, the excess of water is
drained into a larger brook to the south. The pond is not very wide but so deep
that it is hard to see the bottom. Around the pond the vegetation is lush and
green, almost like an oasis in this harsh and infertile part of the Tower Hills.
name: At a Small Pond

desc:
Large, sharp rocks are scattered across the hillside. The vegetation here
consists of lush, green grass and small thorny bushes. To the east the hillside
is so steep that any attempt to climb it would be futile. The rocky hills
continue in all directions. In the north-west you see the contours of a high
tower.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
A small brook flows through lush greenery and sharp pointy rocks. The brook is
not so deep and you can easily see the bottom which is filled with small white
stones. The brook flows in a westerly direction and seems to end in a small pond
just west of here. Looking south you see a very high cliff that points towards
the sky.
name: A Small Brook

desc:
As if tossed around by a playful giant, many large rocks are scattered all
across the slopes of the mighty hills. Large thorny bushes make travel somewhat
painful as their sharp thorns get stuck in every exposed part of your body. The
grass that grows here is quite high, almost reaching a normal man's waist.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
A couple of large trees growing on the top of this hill distinguish it from the
surrounding hills. Growing by the side of the trees are some small bushes which
make this a quite comfortable spot to sit down and rest. The bushes grow in a
half-circle around a large patch of soft green grass.
name: At Some Trees

desc:
Large boulders are scattered across the landscape. Between the boulders, small
trees and lush green bushes try to survive in the infertile soil. To the south
you can catch a glimpse of water, but everywhere else you look, all you can see
are hills and more hills.
name: Tower Hills

desc:
Like a tower, this cliff stands tall and mighty between two bodies of water. The
flat rock is smoothed by time and doesn't even have a single small crack that
could make climbing on top of it a bit easier. However, the top of the cliff is
flat and some soil has been able to get a grip somehow. The view from this
vantage point is astonishing.
name: Cliff

desc:
The ground here is covered by a multitude of small, round stones. Some tufts of
grass and a few bushes grow between the stones. Occasional animal sounds can be
heard from within the bushes. To the east and south you can see a large road
pass through the terrain. To the north and east however, you vision is
completely filled with the mighty Tower Hills.
name: Hills by the Road

desc:
From this rocky hill, more rocky hills can be seen in all directions. There is a
very limited set of wildlife and flora here. Despite the desolate appearance of
the area, tough animals and hardy vegetation can make a living here. A steep
hillside completely blocks your way to the north.
name: Tower Hills

desc:
Stones of all sizes cover the hillsides. Many green bushes grow among the
stones. From within the bushes various sounds, most likely made by birds and
rodents, can be heard. To the south the hillside is so steep that it is
impossible to travel that way.
name: Hills

desc:
The trail runs here in a straight north-south direction. A large oak tree has
somehow managed to get a grip in the infertile landscape, providing some shade
on hot summer days. The only other vegetation are small bushes and occasional
patches of grass.
name: A Small Trail in the Hills

desc:
Large, spiked boulders cover the hills, forcing you to pick your way between
them. The ground is covered with lush, green grass and low shrubs and bushes.
The wind is quite harsh here, tugging at your clothes and making walking upright
a hard task.
name: Tower Hills

desc:
Small, round stones cover the slopes of these hills. Occasional bushes and
shrubs grow between the stones. To the south the hillside is so steep that it is
impossible to travel that way. On the top of the hill to the north you can spot
the remains of a small hut.
name: Tower Hills

desc:
The trail is bending north and west here. To the west you catch a glimpse of a
high tower stretching towards the sky. The terrain beside the trail is rough and
nothing except small bushes grows here. To the south the hills appear to become
lower.
name: A Small Trail in the Hills

desc:
Here the trail is bending east and south. In the far south you see a large road
that seems to be used on a regular basis by travellers. To the west you can
distinguish a high tower rising towards the sky. The hills in the east do not
seem as impassable as those in the west.
name: A Small Trail in the Hills

desc:
The ruined remains of a small hut are the only thing that breaks the monotony of
large boulders and stone blocks. The only vegetation that seems to be able to
get a grip in the sparse soil are small, thorny bushes and occasional low pines.
Looking west, a small trail can be seen, winding its way through the mighty
Tower Hills.
name: At a Ruined Hut

desc:
A small trail is winding its way through the Tower Hills. It seems relatively
well used and travel is easy here. Large bushes grow along the path and sounds
of small animals can be heard from within them. Looking around, you see that you
are completely surrounded by the Tower Hills.
name: A Small Trail in the Hills

desc:
A small trail runs here, making passage through the Tower Hills easier. A few
small bushes grow beside the trail and some tufts of grass grow on its surface.
To the south a large road passes by and to the east and west the Tower Hills
stretch out.
name: A Small Trail in the Hills

desc:
Small bushes grow on this steep hill, which lies just north of a large road.
Among the bushes, many small rocks covered with green moss lie. An occasional
tree can be spotted but the harsh wind prevents them from growing high and
mighty. To the north you can distinguish a small trail winding its way through
the hills.
name: A Steep Hill beside the Road

desc:
The small trail bends north and east here, winding its way through the mighty
Tower Hills. Far off to the west a tower stretches towards the sky, making the
hills around look small and insignificant. Steep hillsides make it hard, but not
impossible, to leave the trail.
name: A Small Trail in the Hills

desc:
The trail bends west and south here, following the steep hillsides. A few large
bushes grow by the trail, but otherwise this landscape provides no place to
hide, escaping the wild predators that roam these hills. In the south a large
road can be discerned.
name: A Small Trail in the Hills

desc:
The wall of stone extends from north to south. Every now and then some of the
stones have fallen down. Some down the cliff on the plains below and some lay
now beneath this side of the wall. To the south the wall turns east and would
prevent entry down the hill, but a portion of it has collapsed into a large pile
of rubble. Foundations of a building are buried in the ground. Maybe it was a
stable or a lodging for men, you will never know. Ground is quite level, but in
the east your view is prevented by a small mound. A juniper bush spreads its
fragrant aroma on top of it.
name: Old Outpost

desc:
The ground slopes down southward from a high hill to the north between a tall
ridge west and a tall hill to the east. Many juniper bushes grow here and there.
At the foot of the slope thick vegetation prevents entry to the grassy plains
below.
name: Grassy Slope

desc:
Upon this large hill, a small campfire burns without creating much smoke. A
small pile of firewood has been left next to the fire, while a few poles have
been put into the ground, forming the pillars of a small tent. A felled tree
seems to be being used as bench upon which, someone has left some fine carvings.
It looks as if this camp has been in use for quite some time.
name: Small Camp

desc:
Junipers grow wild on these hills and the aroma is most delightfull. Down below
the trail winds like a snake around this very hill. It's not possible to enter
it from here unfortunately. The drop down is at least 4 fathoms. The hill slopes
steadily upwards to the north. The edge of the cliff turns westward in the south
and there it drops down to a narrow vale. To the west a small ridge blocks the
view, but it is not an obstacle for a hardy adventurer.
name: Tall Hill

desc:
Junipers grow wild on these hills, and their aroma is most delightful. Down
below, the trail winds like a snake around this very hill. Unfortunately it is
not possible to descend to the trail from here, and the drop down to it is at
least four fathoms. The hill slopes southward. West behind a small depression is
another hilltop.
name: Tall Hill

desc:
The grass grows thick upon this hill top. It looks as if it was the highest
hill. Though the hilltops to the west and east seem to be about the same level.
An old well, now useless and blocked by some stones, stands amidst the
vegetation. To the north the hillside is too steep even for a mountain goat to
climb down. Southward the the hill slopes down and is covered by juniper bushes.
Maybe there is a shelter from the wind.
name: Upon a Tall Grassy Hill

desc:
A narrow tunnel winds its way up and through the hillside. It is wide enough for
three to walk abreast and high enough to lead a horse. Over the years flowing
water has worn deep grooves into the ground making footing difficult for both
horse and rider. The floor is covered by puddles, mud and slippery stones. The
